---
title: "DRS vs Active Aero"
subtitle: "Did DRS make overtaking artificial — and does 2026 show a difference?"
---

## The narrative

> *"DRS made overtaking artificial — it turned genuine racing moves into highway passes
> where the chasing driver simply drove past on the straight. Removing it should restore
> real racing."*

The hypothesis predicts that overtaking activity should be lower without DRS than with it.
The 2026 regulation change — replacing DRS with active aerodynamics — provides a natural
experiment, comparing Ground Effect Era (DRS on, 2022–2025) against 2026 (active aero).

---

## Method

The overtake index is computed for every race across both eras and compared. Because 2026
has only 3 completed rounds at the time of writing, this comparison is **directional only**.

```{python}
import sys
sys.path.insert(0, '../..')

import pandas as pd
import plotly.graph_objects as go
import scripts.export_data as exp
from src.analysis.overtake_index import overtake_index_by_era

race_oi_2022 = exp.read('overtake_index_by_race')
results_2026 = exp.read('results_2026')

from src.analysis.overtake_index import overtake_index_per_race
race_oi_2026 = overtake_index_per_race(results_2026)

import pandas as pd
combined_oi = pd.concat([race_oi_2022, race_oi_2026], ignore_index=True)
era_summary = overtake_index_by_era(combined_oi)
print(era_summary.to_string(index=False))
```

```{python}
#| fig-cap: "Overtake index per race coloured by era. Hover to see circuit and round detail."

from src.utils.era_helper import get_era_name
combined_oi['era'] = combined_oi['season'].apply(get_era_name)

ERA_COLOURS = {
    'Ground Effect Era': '#636EFA',
    '2026 Era': '#FF6B35',
}

fig = go.Figure()

for era, colour in ERA_COLOURS.items():
    sub = combined_oi[combined_oi['era'] == era]
    fig.add_trace(go.Scatter(
        x=sub['season'],
        y=sub['overtake_index'],
        mode='markers',
        name=era,
        marker=dict(color=colour, size=8, opacity=0.8),
        customdata=sub[['race_name', 'season', 'round', 'positions_gained', 'starters']].values,
        hovertemplate=(
            '<b>%{customdata[0]}</b><br>'
            '%{customdata[1]} R%{customdata[2]}<br>'
            'Overtake index: %{y:.3f}<br>'
            'Positions gained: %{customdata[3]} / %{customdata[4]} starters'
            '<extra></extra>'
        ),
    ))

# Era mean lines — annotations offset vertically so they don't overlap
annotation_yshifts = {'Ground Effect Era': 10, '2026 Era': -16}
for era, colour in ERA_COLOURS.items():
    sub = combined_oi[combined_oi['era'] == era]
    mean_oi = sub['overtake_index'].mean()
    x_min = sub['season'].min()
    x_max = sub['season'].max()
    fig.add_shape(
        type='line',
        x0=x_min - 0.3, x1=x_max + 0.3,
        y0=mean_oi, y1=mean_oi,
        line=dict(color=colour, width=2, dash='dash'),
    )
    fig.add_annotation(
        x=x_max + 0.3, y=mean_oi,
        text=f'{era} mean: {mean_oi:.3f}',
        showarrow=False,
        xanchor='left',
        font=dict(color=colour, size=11),
        yshift=annotation_yshifts[era],
    )

all_seasons = sorted(combined_oi['season'].unique())
fig.update_layout(
    title='Overtake Index per Race — Ground Effect Era vs 2026 (Active Aero)',
    xaxis=dict(
        title='Season',
        tickvals=all_seasons,
        ticktext=[str(s) for s in all_seasons],
    ),
    yaxis_title='Overtake index (positions gained / starters)',
    template='plotly_dark',
    height=500,
    margin=dict(t=60, r=160, b=100),
    legend=dict(
        orientation='h',
        yanchor='top',
        y=-0.15,
        xanchor='center',
        x=0.5,
    ),
)
fig.show()
```

---

## Findings

| Question | Finding |
|---|---|
| Mean overtake index — Ground Effect Era (DRS, 2022–2025)? | 1.688 across 92 races |
| Mean overtake index — 2026 Era (active aero, 3 rounds)? | 1.874 across 3 rounds |
| Does removing DRS appear to reduce position changes? | No — 2026 shows a *higher* index |
| Is the "DRS made overtaking artificial" narrative supported? | Not supported |

**Verdict: Not supported — on 3 rounds of data.**

If DRS were artificially inflating overtaking, its removal should produce fewer position
changes. Instead the opposite is observed: early 2026 races show a higher overtake index
than the DRS era average.

Two important caveats apply. First, **3 rounds is a very small sample** — the 2026 figure
is directional only and must be revisited as the season matures. Second, the early 2026
calendar (Australia, China, Japan) may skew the comparison — these tracks produce different
overtaking profiles to the full calendar average.

The data currently challenges the narrative, but a full season is needed before drawing
firm conclusions.
